description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both with a dark, weathered metallic finish. The axe head is mounted onto a sturdy wooden handle, which is reinforced with golden metallic bands at the top and two dark leather straps near the lower end for improved grip. The handle is straight and cylindrical, showing a natural wood grain texture, and the overall construction suggests durability and strength. The blade edges are slightly curved, giving the axe a menacing and functional appearance, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or display. The metallic head appears to be securely fastened to the handle, with no visible gaps or looseness. The overall aesthetic is rugged and utilitarian, with a hint of medieval or fantasy influence.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a dark, weathered metal blade and a straight wooden handle reinforced by golden bands and leather straps. The design is robust and visually striking, blending utility with a medieval flair.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical axe blades, the golden band at the top, and the full length of the wooden handle with leather straps.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the axe blades and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the opposite faces of the double blades and the handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with visible grain, reinforced by two dark leather straps and a golden metallic band at the top; designed for a secure grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed symmetrical axe blade with broad, curved edges and a dark, weathered finish; no beard or poll, functional and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metallic band reinforcement at the top.
